experience.description: Initiated and implemented data collection and cost analysis for special projects, leveraging strong analytical skills to drive process improvements and ensure accurate documentation for the Division of Safety and Operations team. Coordinated event logistics and digital communications, handling setup and cleanup while designing flyers, web content, and promotional materials with CSS and Google Cloud, demonstrating technical aptitude and multi-tasking to boost campus event attendance and improve audience engagement.

Performed preliminary analysis of online sales data using PowerBI and SQL Server, uncovering key sales patterns that guided the team’s next reporting cycle. Generated pivot tables in Excel, performed data conversion and conducted measurement analysis to compare sales performance across product categories, revealing that the top 3 best-selling products accounted for 60% of total revenue and enabling focused resource prioritization.

Analyzed loan information using statistical modeling techniques and designed a comprehensive KPI dashboard with PowerBI, leveraging SQL and data mining techniques to enable banking clients to monitor loan performance and identify repayment patterns, resulting in improved reporting efficiency and positive client feedback.
Implemented a web-based virtual tourist guide application using React, which provided interactive maps with location markers and directions, enhancing user navigation experience.

from_resume.name: Air Pollution and Economic Activity - World Bank Data Lab Capstone Project
from_resume.description: Analyze and integrate satellite air pollution (NO2, PM2.5, SO2, CH4) and sectoral GDP datasets using Python to evaluate pollution as a proxy for economic activity across countries as part of a World Bank Data Lab affiliated capstone project at GWU, collaborating with economics researchers.

from_resume.name: Multi-Agent Job Email Assistant
from_resume.description: Built a multi-agent system of automation by means of LangGraph and an agentic RAG (Retrieval, Augmented Generation) pipeline to convert disorganized Gmail data into a structured job tracker, thus enabling querying in natural language through ChromaDB. Formulated a streamlined classifier which scored 99% accuracy by employing TF-IDF and Logistic Regression to remove the inbox clutter, and wrapped up the whole thing into a Streamlit dashboard linked to the Gmail API for live updates.

from_resume.name: Image Steganalysis: Detect Hidden Information with Machine Learning
from_resume.description: Developed an image analysis system with a team of four in 36 hours to detect hidden messages in images, achieving 95% accuracy using machine learning models.

from_resume.name: Dating Platform Behavior and Prediction Analysis
from_resume.description: Collaborated with team of four to create dating match platform model using R to analyze 60K+ rows of raw data. Examined user data to discover that users with graduate degrees selected hiking as a first-date activity nearly 45% more often than those with only a bachelor’s degree. Formulated 15+ ggplot2 visualizations revealing demographic-based match acceptance patterns, enabling team to identify areas for optimization and generate a report for data insights.

from_resume.name: Virtual Police Station
from_resume.description: Created web application accessible through mobile to enable better communication between victims and police stations, capable of accepting full incident data and complainant details with 60% lesser input compared to conventional reporting, and capable of lessening documentation procedure by up to 15 minutes per case through interface usability analysis.

from_resume.name: Eye on the Road
from_resume.description: Led the development of a web application using MapMyIndia API to enable users to identify potholes on travel routes with a view to improve the journey planning and possibly save 15 minutes of travel inconvenience.

from_github.name: Bitcamp_Steganalysis
from_github.readme:
We've developed a groundbreaking model that can instantly determine if an image contains a hidden message. With 95% accuracy, our technology analyzes digital images to detect subtle steganographic traces that are invisible to the human eye.
Imagine being able to scan through thousands of images and identify which ones might be concealing secret information - that's exactly what our model does. Perfect for cybersecurity, digital forensics, and protecting sensitive information.
In just milliseconds, we can tell you: Is there a secret message hiding in this image? Yes or no.

Contributions in : https://github.com/aniruddhg43986683/Bitcamp_Steganalysis/tree/main

from_github.name: Food-Deserts-in-the-United-States
from_github.readme:
🥦 Food Desert Analysis Across the United States

This project explores the distribution, severity, and key socio-economic drivers of food deserts in the United States using the USDA's Food Research Atlas and Food Environment Atlas datasets. Through in-depth data analysis, visualization, and machine learning models, we identify high-risk regions and propose actionable solutions for improving food accessibility and reducing nutritional inequality.

---

📁 Project Structure

- 📊 Data Exploration & Visualization
- 🧠 Predictive Modeling (Classification & Regression)
- 🧪 Statistical Testing (T-tests, Correlation Analysis)
- 📈 Policy Recommendations & Regional Insights

---

🧾 Datasets Used

- Food Access Research Atlas (2019)  
  Provided by the USDA. Includes tract-level information on low-income and low-access areas.

- Food Environment Atlas  
  County-level indicators of food choices, health outcomes, and community characteristics.

---

🧪 Modeling

- Classification Model:
  - Type: Rand

from_github.name: InvestIQ
from_github.description: A UI/UX-first educational web app that teaches beginners how real investing works — honestly, visually, and interactively.
from_github.readme:
# 📊 InvestIQ – Interactive Portfolio Sandbox for Beginner Investors  

## 🌟 Inspiration  
Most beginner investors have **no idea** what cash, bonds, stocks, index funds, or crypto actually mean — or how risky they are during real crashes.  
Almost every financial tool assumes prior knowledge, which leaves beginners confused and overwhelmed.  

We wanted to fix that by building a **beginner-first**, **visual**, **game-like** investing education tool.  
A tool where users *learn by doing*, not reading boring textbooks.  

Thus, **InvestIQ** was born.  

---

## 🧩 What it does  
InvestIQ is a **multi-page, interactive financial learning platform** that teaches beginners how investing works using:  
- 🎮 **Drag-and-drop portfolio building**  
- 📊 **Live animated pie charts**  
- ⚠️ **Real-time risk calculation**  
- 🧠 **Beginner-friendly explanations**  
- 📰 **Real market event stories with external links**  
- 🌈 **Cyberpunk neon UI/UX and animations**  

### 💡 Core Features  
- 🧱 Drag mone

from_github.name: Multi-Agent-Job-Email-Assistant
from_github.readme:
# Multi Agent Gmail Job Application Tracker
#### Automated Email Classification • Job Entity Extraction • RAG Search • ChromaDB Storage • Streamlit Demo

## DATS 6312 – Introduction to Natural Language Processing  
George Washington University  
Instructor: Dr. Amir Jafari  
Group Members: Aswin Balaji Thippa Ramesh, Siddharth Sriram, Rahul Arvind

# Project Overview

This repository contains the full implementation of our final NLP project for DATS 6312 at The George Washington University.  
The project focuses on designing and deploying an automated, end-to-end system that:

- Connects securely to the user’s Gmail inbox  
- Classifies incoming emails as job-related or non-job-related  
- Extracts structured metadata such as company name, position applied for, status, application date, and original Gmail link  
- Stores processed emails as embeddings inside a vector database (ChromaDB)  
- Enables a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) interface to query job application history using

code_quality.summary: The code is generally clean, readable, and well-structured with clear separation of concerns (training vs. prediction). Error handling for file operations is present and functional. However, there are some inconsistencies in path management across different classifier modules, and opportunities exist to refactor repetitive code and improve configurability.

technical_complexity.summary: The project demonstrates a strong understanding of various NLP and machine learning techniques. It progresses from traditional ML (TF-IDF with Logistic Regression in Classifier 1), to a hybrid approach combining sentence embeddings and rule-based regex patterns (Classifier 2), and finally to state-of-the-art deep learning with Hugging Face Transformers (DistilBERT in Classifier 3). This range of approaches indicates significant technical breadth and depth.

architecture_patterns: Machine Learning Pipeline (Data Loading -> Preprocessing -> Model Training/Loading -> Prediction)
Modular Design (separation of training and prediction scripts, helper functions)
Data Access Layer (simple file I/O for CSV/Excel)
Configuration Management (basic hardcoded paths and thresholds, improved in Classifier 3 with PROJECT_ROOT)
skills_demonstrated: Python Programming (scripting, data manipulation)
Data Handling and Cleaning (Pandas for Excel/CSV, fillna, column operations)
Traditional Machine Learning (Scikit-learn, TF-IDF, Logistic Regression, model evaluation)
Natural Language Processing (Text preprocessing, regex, TF-IDF, sentence embeddings, Transformer models)
Deep Learning Fundamentals (PyTorch for model inference, Hugging Face Transformers for fine-tuning)
Software Engineering Fundamentals (basic error handling, modularity, file system operations)
Model Persistence and Loading (joblib)
Experimentation and Iterative Development (implied by multiple classification approaches)
best_practices_used: Modular Code Design (e.g., dedicated safe loading functions)
Clear Separation of Concerns (training vs. prediction scripts)
Basic Error Handling and Validation (os.path.exists, try-except for file I/O, column validation)
Dataset Balancing (Classifier 3 training script explicitly balances classes for imbalanced datasets)
areas_for_improvement: **Path Management:** Standardize and centralize path configurations, ideally using `pathlib` for cross-platform compatibility instead of `os.path.join` and relative `../../` paths. Consider a dedicated `config.py` file.
**Code Duplication:** Refactor repetitive data preprocessing steps (e.g., `fillna('')`, column concatenation) into reusable utility functions or a shared module.
**Logging:** Replace `print()` statements with Python's built-in `logging` module for more robust and configurable logging.
**Error Handling:** In a larger application, consider raising custom exceptions instead of `sys.exit(1)` to allow for more graceful error recovery or handling by calling code.
**Configurability:** Externalize hardcoded model names, file paths, and thresholds into configuration files or command-line arguments for easier modification and deployment.
**Model Management:** Implement a more robust `EmailClassifierWrapper` in Classifier 3, potentially encapsulating the `predict` method directly.
**Testing:** Add unit and integration tests to ensure code correctness and maintainability.
**Documentation:** Enhance docstrings for functions, classes, and modules to improve code understanding and maintainability.
**Input Validation:** Beyond column existence, consider validating data types and content where appropriate.
**Version Control for ML Assets:** For a more mature ML project, consider tools like DVC or MLflow for tracking data, models, and experiments.
code_insights.overall_assessment: The project demonstrates a solid foundation in machine learning and natural language processing, showcasing the ability to implement and compare diverse classification techniques. The code is functional and addresses the core problem of email classification effectively. While good practices are present for data handling and model management, there are clear opportunities to enhance the codebase towards more robust, scalable, and production-ready software engineering standards, particularly in configuration management, code organization, and error handling. The multi-classifier approach is a strong indicator of an experimental and solution-oriented mindset.
notable_observations: **Multi-Strategy Classification:** The most striking feature is the development of three distinct classifiers (traditional ML, semantic similarity with regex, and deep learning with Transformers), indicating a comprehensive approach to the problem.
**Evolution of Techniques:** The project showcases a clear progression in technical sophistication, from simpler `sklearn` models to advanced `huggingface/transformers` usage.

**Domain-Specific Rules:** Classifier 2 effectively integrates domain knowledge through explicit regex patterns for common job-related email phrases, which can significantly improve accuracy for specific categories.
**Awareness of Data Imbalance:** The training script for Classifier 3 demonstrates awareness of imbalanced datasets and applies balancing techniques (sampling), which is a crucial ML best practice.

**Project Name vs. Code Samples:** The project name 'Multi-Agent-Job-Email-Assistant' suggests a broader architecture, but the provided code snippets focus exclusively on the email classification component, implying these are building blocks for a larger system.

code_quality.summary: The PHP code exhibits several critical quality issues, most notably severe security vulnerabilities like SQL injection and plain-text password storage. Input validation is minimal and ineffective. There's a lack of separation of concerns, with business logic and presentation heavily intertwined. JavaScript code for the frontend is functional but primarily relies on integrating third-party jQuery plugins without demonstrating complex custom logic. Overall, the code quality is poor, particularly on the backend.

technical_complexity.summary: The technical complexity is low. The backend logic involves basic form submission handling, direct database insertions/selections, and session management. There are no advanced data structures, algorithms, or design patterns employed. The frontend uses standard jQuery and common plugins, which reduces the complexity of custom JavaScript development.

skills_demonstrated: Basic PHP syntax and control structures (if/else, isset)
Handling HTTP POST requests and form data
Basic SQL queries (INSERT, SELECT)
Basic database interaction (mysqli or PDO object usage, mysqli_real_escape_string)
HTML5 structure and meta-tags
CSS framework integration (Bootstrap)
JavaScript (jQuery) for DOM manipulation and UI enhancements
Integration of third-party jQuery plugins (meanmenu, tooltips, sticky, NiceScroll, jQuery Validate)
Basic session management
best_practices_used: Use of 'mysqli_real_escape_string' (though not sufficient and only used in one file)
Client-side validation with jQuery Validate (though not fully leveraged in server-side handling)
Responsive design considerations (viewport meta tag, Bootstrap, meanmenu plugin)
areas_for_improvement: **Security (Critical Priority)**:

-   **Prevent SQL Injection**: Implement prepared statements with parameterized queries for *all* database interactions (`mysqli_prepare` / `PDO::prepare`). The current use of direct variable insertion and `mysqli_real_escape_string` is insufficient and inconsistent.
-   **Password Hashing**: Store and verify passwords using secure hashing algorithms (e.g., `password_hash()` and `password_verify()` in PHP) instead of plain text.

-   **Input Sanitization and Validation**: Implement robust server-side validation for all user inputs (e.g., check for empty fields individually, data types, length, format for email/phone). Client-side validation is for UX, not security.
**Code Structure and Maintainability**:
-   **Separation of Concerns**: Decouple PHP logic from HTML presentation. Consider using a templating engine or a simple MVC-like structure.
-   **Centralized Configuration**: Consolidate database connection details and ensure consistent connection object usage (e.g., `$conn` vs `$con`).
-   **Consistent Redirection**: Use `header('Location: ...')` for all PHP-initiated redirects instead of JavaScript.
-   **Error Handling**: Provide more specific and user-friendly error messages, and implement server-side error logging.
**Database Interaction**:
-   **PDO**: Consider migrating to PDO for a more consistent and flexible database abstraction layer.
**Frontend**:
-   **Consolidate JavaScript**: Combine multiple `$(document).ready()` calls into a single block for better organization and potentially minor performance gains.
code_insights.overall_assessment: This repository demonstrates a foundational understanding of web development using PHP, HTML, CSS, and JavaScript with jQuery. However, the backend code has severe security vulnerabilities (SQL injection, plain-text passwords) and lacks robust input validation, which are critical flaws for any web application. The architectural approach is simple and monolithic. While the frontend integrates various jQuery plugins effectively for UI, the core PHP logic requires significant refactoring to meet modern security and quality standards. This project serves as a basic starting point but is not suitable for production without major security and quality enhancements.
notable_observations: Inconsistent database connection variable names (`$conn` in 'index.php' and 'register.php', `$con` in 'login.php').
Inconsistent use of `require` vs `include` for configuration files.
The inclusion of 'jquery.validate.js' suggests an intent for client-side validation, but it's not mirrored by robust server-side validation in the provided PHP snippets.
The 'index.php' file is a mix of PHP logic and the start of an HTML document, implying it's a 'header' or entry point for a larger, similarly structured application.

from_github.name: rolematrix
from_github.readme:
# Role Matrix - Resume Screening Application

An intelligent resume screening tool that matches candidate skills against job descriptions and provides interview recommendations.

## Features

✅ **Multi-file Resume Upload** - Process multiple PDF/DOCX resumes at once

✅ **Skill Extraction** - Automatically extracts 300+ technical and soft skills

✅ **Job Description Parsing** - Analyzes JD requirements

✅ **Confidence Scoring** - Calculates match percentage

✅ **Interview Recommendations** - Auto-categorizes: Interview, Maybe, Not now

✅ **Alternate Role Suggestions** - Suggests better-fitting roles for low-match candidates

✅ **Candidate Profiles** - Exports structured JSON profiles

✅ **Visual Dashboard** - Color-coded results table

## Setup Instructions

### 1. Navigate to Project Directory
```bash
cd /Users/dhanushmathivanan/dhanush_gwu/gwu_venture_competition/role-matrix/rolematrix
```

### 2. Activate Virtual Environment
```bash
source venv/bin/activate  # On macOS/Linux
# OR
ven

from_github.name: Virtual-Police-Station

# 🚔 Virtual Police Station - Tamil Nadu Government

A comprehensive web-based platform for online complaint registration and management system built with modern web technologies. This digital solution brings transparency, efficiency, and accessibility to law enforcement services for the citizens of Tamil Nadu.

## ✨ Features

### 🏛️ **Government-Grade Security & Compliance**
- Official Tamil Nadu Government themed interface
- Secure user authentication with password hashing
- Session management and data protection
- Multi-language support (Tamil, Hindi, English)

### 👥 **Dual User System**
- **Guest Complaints**: File complaints without registration
- **Registered Users**: Enhanced tracking and dashboard access
- **Admin Panel**: Complete complaint management system
- **Role-based access control**

### 📱 **Modern User Experience**
- Responsive design that works on all devices
- Real-time complaint status tracking
- Interactive dashboard with statistics
- Evidence photo upload with prev

code_quality.summary: The code is generally readable and uses clear variable names, indicating an intermediate understanding of PHP. It leverages `require_once` for configuration and includes basic error handling with `try-catch` blocks for database operations. However, there's a lack of consistent input sanitization across all user-supplied data, the architecture is largely procedural without strict separation of concerns, and debugging `error_log` statements are present in what appears to be production code. Code quality is also hampered by several security vulnerabilities and the presence of utility scripts that should be secured or removed.

technical_complexity.summary: The application's technical complexity is relatively low. It primarily involves standard CRUD (Create, Read, Update, Delete) operations for complaints and admin users, basic session-based authentication, and file uploads. There are no complex algorithms, advanced data structures, or intricate design patterns employed. The logic for each page is straightforward and follows a linear execution flow.

skills_demonstrated: PHP (procedural programming)
PDO for database interaction (MySQL/MariaDB)
SQL query writing (SELECT, UPDATE, Aggregate Functions like COUNT, SUM, CASE)
HTML/CSS integration (implied)
Session Management
Form Handling and Validation (basic)
Strong Password Hashing and Verification (password_hash, password_verify)
File Uploads (basic implementation)
Basic error handling with try-catch blocks
best_practices_used: Strong password hashing using `password_hash()` and `password_verify()` for admin authentication.
Use of prepared statements for sensitive database write operations (`UPDATE`, `INSERT`) to mitigate SQL injection.
Session-based authentication for controlling admin access.
Basic input trimming (`trim()`) for user-submitted data.
Crucial `is_uploaded_file()` check to validate file uploads.
Basic access control (`if (!isset($_SESSION['admin_id']))`) for authenticated areas.
areas_for_improvement: **Comprehensive Input Validation and Sanitization:** Implement robust validation and sanitization for all user inputs (e.g., complaint descriptions, remarks, email, phone) to prevent XSS attacks and ensure data integrity. Escaping output when displaying user-generated content is critical.

**Robust File Upload Security:** Enhance file upload security by validating MIME types, enforcing file size limits, sanitizing filenames to prevent malicious file execution (e.g., double extensions) and directory traversal, and storing uploaded files outside the web root or with randomized, non-guessable filenames.
**CSRF Protection:** Implement CSRF tokens for all POST requests to protect against Cross-Site Request Forgery attacks, especially for critical actions like updating complaint status.

**Improved Error Handling and User Feedback:** Remove debug-specific `error_log` statements from production code. Provide more user-friendly error messages instead of raw database errors, and implement a consistent error logging strategy.

**Code Organization and Architecture:** Refactor the code to separate concerns more effectively, potentially moving towards a basic MVC pattern or at least using functions/classes for business logic, data access, and presentation to improve maintainability and scalability.

**Secure Utility Scripts:** The `create_admin.php` script, which hardcodes a default admin password, should be removed or highly secured (e.g., behind authentication or only accessible via CLI) after initial setup to prevent unauthorized admin creation/reset.

**Consistent Database Interaction:** Adopt a consistent approach to database interaction, preferably using prepared statements for all queries, even simple `SELECT` statements, as a robust security practice.
**Configuration Management:** Centralize and secure sensitive configurations, ensuring default credentials or setup-specific information are not publicly accessible or hardcoded in deployed code.
code_insights.overall_assessment: The 'Virtual-Police-Station' project demonstrates a foundational understanding of PHP, SQL, and web application development. It successfully implements core functionalities like admin login with strong password hashing and complaint submission. While the project is a good starting point, it exhibits several critical security vulnerabilities, particularly in input validation, sanitization, and file upload handling, which would make it unsuitable for a production environment without significant security hardening. The architectural style is predominantly procedural, which is functional for small projects but lacks the maintainability and scalability benefits of more modern patterns. It represents an intermediate developer's attempt at building a web application, strong in some areas (password security) but weak in others (input/file security).

top_repos.name: Bitcamp_Steganalysis
top_repos.readme_preview:
We've developed a groundbreaking model that can instantly determine if an image contains a hidden message. With 95% accuracy, our technology analyzes digital images to detect subtle steganographic traces that are invisible to the human eye.
Imagine being able to scan through thousands of images and identify which ones might be concealing secret information - that's exactly what our model does. Perfect for cybersecurity, digital forensics, and protecting sensitive information.
In just milliseconds, we can tell you: Is there a secret message hiding in this image? Yes or no.

Contributions in : https://github.com/aniruddhg43986683/Bitcamp_Steganalysis/tree/main

top_repos.name: Food-Deserts-in-the-United-States
top_repos.readme_preview:
🥦 Food Desert Analysis Across the United States

This project explores the distribution, severity, and key socio-economic drivers of food deserts in the United States using the USDA's Food Research Atlas and Food Environment Atlas datasets. Through in-depth data analysis, visualization, and machine learning models, we identify high-risk regions and propose actionable solutions for improving food accessibility and reducing nutritional inequality.

---

📁 Project Structure

- 📊 Data Exploration & Visualization
- 🧠 Predictive Modeling (Classification & Regression)
- 🧪 Statistical Testing (T-tests, Correlation Analysis)
- 📈 Policy Recommendations & Regional Insights

---

🧾 Datasets Used

- Food Access Research Atlas (2019)  
  Provided by the USDA. Includes tract-level information on low-income and low-access areas.

- Food Environment Atlas  
  County-level indicators of food choices, health outcomes, and community characteristics.

---

🧪 Modeling

- Classification Model:
  - Type: Rand

top_repos.name: InvestIQ
top_repos.description: A UI/UX-first educational web app that teaches beginners how real investing works — honestly, visually, and interactively.
top_repos.readme_preview:
# 📊 InvestIQ – Interactive Portfolio Sandbox for Beginner Investors  

## 🌟 Inspiration  
Most beginner investors have **no idea** what cash, bonds, stocks, index funds, or crypto actually mean — or how risky they are during real crashes.  
Almost every financial tool assumes prior knowledge, which leaves beginners confused and overwhelmed.  

We wanted to fix that by building a **beginner-first**, **visual**, **game-like** investing education tool.  
A tool where users *learn by doing*, not reading boring textbooks.  

Thus, **InvestIQ** was born.  

---

## 🧩 What it does  
InvestIQ is a **multi-page, interactive financial learning platform** that teaches beginners how investing works using:  
- 🎮 **Drag-and-drop portfolio building**  
- 📊 **Live animated pie charts**  
- ⚠️ **Real-time risk calculation**  
- 🧠 **Beginner-friendly explanations**  
- 📰 **Real market event stories with external links**  
- 🌈 **Cyberpunk neon UI/UX and animations**  

### 💡 Core Features  
- 🧱 Drag mone
